select_columns.label: Selecting Columns
select_columns.summary: How to select specific columns from a table.

sketch2.title: How should you read the result?

specific_columns.title: Selecting specific columns keeps results focused.
specific_columns.bodyMarkdown:
When you only need a few fields, select only those columns instead of returning everything.

~~~sql
SELECT name, price
FROM products;
~~~

select_star.title: SELECT * returns all columns from the chosen table.
select_star.bodyMarkdown:
The star is useful for quick exploration, but it is often too broad for real reports.

~~~sql
SELECT *
FROM products;
~~~

result_reading.title: Read the result set by checking the selected columns and the returned rows together.
result_reading.bodyMarkdown: Always verify that the columns you selected match the question you were trying to answer.

practice_note.title: Practice by predicting which columns should appear before running the query.
practice_note.bodyMarkdown: Say the desired output in plain English first, then write the SELECT list to match it.

module1_select_columns_practice_sql.title: Practice: Selecting Columns
module1_select_columns_practice_sql.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows only the `name` and `price` columns for five rows.

Then run it.

help.concept: How to select specific columns from a table.
help.hint_2: Use LIMIT 5 to keep the output short.

module1_select_columns_practice_sql.hint: Choose only the columns named in the question.
module1_select_columns_practice_sql.starterCode:
-- Show only the needed columns
SELECT name, price
FROM products
LIMIT 5

module1_select_columns_core_concept.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It is about retrieving only the columns you actually need from a table.

options.a: Selecting specific columns can make a result easier to read.

options.d: Selecting fewer columns can better match a real business question.

module1_select_columns_workflow_order.title: Workflow for Selecting Columns
module1_select_columns_workflow_order.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for Selecting Columns.

help.concept: Choose the data need first, then write the select list, then verify the output.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: identify which columns answer the question
tokens.t2: write those columns in the SELECT list
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the result set
